title: SSRF, XML, Content, Query, Abuse, Secrets, and Retention Defense · Go Build Room
summary: A public-interest newsroom incrementally builds threat-modeled feed-service control set from original RSS, Atom, HTTP, and PostgreSQL fixtures while retaining prior Go, HTTP-client, HTTP-server, SQL, Git, accessibility, security, testing, cancellation, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of threat-modeled feed-service control set while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, ca…
objectives: Trace assets, actors, trust boundaries, entry points, abuse cases, controls, detection, response, and residual risk across every data flow.
Combine URL grammar, allow or deny policy, address validation, redirect checks, proxy controls, egress isolation, metadata blocking, and rebinding tests.
Align wire, decompression, XML, text, HTML sanitation, search, pagination, bulk, and export limits so one stage cannot amplify another.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Go Threat Model
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Go team handles case 4376 during a cross-tenant privacy audit. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove defense-in-depth SSRF, parser and query budgets, secrets, retention, deletion, and residual ownership; untrusted XML and feed content must not cross admission or rendering boundaries; then change one tenant, cursor, reader-state, authorization, cache, or accessibility condition without leaking state. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Trace assets, actors, trust boundaries, entry points, abuse cases, controls, detection, response, and residual risk across every data flow.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Go Threat Model.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for trace assets, actors, trust boundaries, entry points, abuse cases, controls, detection, response, and residual risk across every data flow. For Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Go Threat Model, keep Feed Go Threat Model connected to executable Go feed, XML, HTTP, PostgreSQL, schedule, API, reader, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

options.text: State the Feed Go Threat Model preconditions, preserve earlier contracts, and name the trace or changed-input result that will decide success.
Using HTTPS and parameterized SQL covers the service threat model. That claim does not survive the commit to the mission model evidence boundary.

For commit to the mission model, import one convenient trusted RSS feed, omit reader and publisher outcomes, revision, discovery and SSRF scope, Atom and namespace cases, XML and HTTP budgets, PostgreSQL constraints and transaction, schedule, lease, goroutine cleanup, tenant, accessibility, recovery, and controlled native transfer, then accept plausible rows as feed-service proof.
